title: Qlog QUIC Fixture Hydration Verification
scope: Verify recorded qlog fixture hydration through the sibling import package from the QUIC test project, including contained JSON, sequential JSON Text Sequences, contained CBOR, semantic comparisons of retained-model objects, and rejection of malformed or unsupported input. Explicit round-trip comparisons for the repository-produced serializer outputs remain covered in `tests/Incursa.Qlog.Import.Tests`. The slice does not include replay execution or new runtime import dependencies in `Incursa.Qlog.Quic`.
verification_method: Requirement-homed unit tests, fixture-level semantic assertions, negative fixture corpus checks, and linked import-package round-trip tests.
preconditions: The qlog SpecTrace artifacts validate successfully under the repository's JSON validation script.
The recorded fixtures are present under the QUIC test project fixture corpus.
The sibling import package exists and exposes the planned import entry points.
procedure: Run scripts/Validate-SpecTraceJson.ps1 over the qlog canonical artifacts.
Run the QUIC fixture hydration tests that load contained JSON, sequential JSON Text Sequences, and contained CBOR fixtures.
Confirm semantic assertions on hydrated retained-model objects and opaque extension data.
Exercise malformed and unsupported fixture inputs to confirm the importer fails cleanly.
Run the sibling import package tests to cover the explicit round-trip proof across the supported artifact shapes.
expected_result: The QUIC fixture tests prove the importer can hydrate recorded qlog artifacts without contaminating production code, and malformed or unsupported input fails clearly.
